assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains an 'Acceptance Criteria Details' table with 5 rows, all marked PASS: (1) single license filter MIT, (2) comma-separated MIT,Apache-2.0, (3) INVALID-999 returns 400, (4) filter integrates with pagination, (5) response shape unchanged PaginatedResults<PackageSummary>. The main summary table also shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each contain 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file as specified'. This confirms all 3 files (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are within the task's specified scope.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR'. The closing paragraph states 'No review feedback to process.' and 'No sub-tasks or root-cause investigation required.' No evidence of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs consist of report.md (the verification report) and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md (detailed per-criterion analysis). All output files are report/analysis documents. None of the outputs contain diffs, patches, or code modifications. The report only references reading and analyzing the PR diff, not modifying it.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md presents its findings in a summary table format with 'Overall: PASS' as a conclusion but does not execute or recommend any merge action. The report ends with '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr v0.11.0.*' indicating it is a comment/report for human consumption, not an auto-merge action.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. The closing paragraph also states 'No security concerns detected.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | New test file `tests/api/package.rs` adds 4 integration tests covering all acceptance criteria'. The value is ADDITIVE and identifies the new test file.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table contains all required verdicts: Intent Alignment domain — Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (N/A); Security domain — Sensitive Patterns (PASS); Correctness domain — CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); Style/Conventions domain — Test Quality (PASS, with detail 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected; Eval Quality: N/A') combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality, and Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). All expected verdicts from all four domains are present.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Beyond the summary table, report.md includes: (1) Intent Alignment: 'PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file' and detailed description of endpoint/service/test layers; (2) Security: 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'; (3) Correctness: Full 'Acceptance Criteria Details' table with 5 rows, each containing specific code-level evidence (e.g., 'validate_license_param parses MIT, service filters with is_in(["MIT"]) via inner join; test test_list_packages_single_license_filter verifies 2 MIT packages returned from 3 seeded'); (4) Style/Conventions: 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected'. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive per-criterion analysis with code snippets, method names, and test verification details.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md Test Quality cell states: 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected; Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A within the Test Quality combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (as shown in Test Quality cell of report.md). No evidence of eval failure sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs. The report states 'No sub-tasks or root-cause investigation required.'

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table shows: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments exist on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as top-level rows in the summary table, separate from the domain-specific checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, etc.), indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts independent from domain analysis.
